title: [Bug]: Message metadata label defaults accept control characters
summary: Message metadata label defaults can include control characters when configured as strings or arrays.
observed: MessageMetadataLabelsConfig and MessageMetadataRuleConfig accept label defaults containing ASCII control characters.
expected: Configured labels should reject control characters before they can reach envelopes, files, Oracle columns, or metrics.
reproduction: Construct MessageMetadataLabelsConfig(header='Labels', default=['ops\u0000label']) and observe validation succeeds.
failing_test: Add unit coverage for global and rule label defaults containing ASCII control characters.
impact: Unsafe labels can be persisted broadly and can create log or terminal hygiene problems when operators inspect records.
security: Labels are operator-controlled metadata and should follow allow-list style validation.
acceptance: Global label defaults reject ASCII control characters.
Subject rule label defaults reject ASCII control characters.
Label validation keeps duplicate removal and blank-label behavior intact.
Focused tests cover the invalid cases.
tests: Run focused metadata boundary tests and full validation.
documentation: Update release notes and test report.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
